records.ety: [Pref. in- not + direct: cf. F. indirect.]

defs: Not direct; not straight or rectilinear; deviating from a direct line or course; circuitous; as, an indirect road.
Not tending to an aim, purpose, or result by the plainest course, or by obvious means, but obliquely or consequentially; by remote means; as, an indirect accusation, attack, answer, or proposal.
quotes: By what bypaths and indirect, crooked ways I met this crown.

defs: Not straightforward or upright; unfair; dishonest; tending to mislead or deceive.
quotes: Indirect dealing will be discovered one time or other.

defs: Not resulting directly from an act or cause, but more or less remotely connected with or growing out of it; as, indirect results, damages, or claims.
Not reaching the end aimed at by the most plain and direct method; as, an indirect proof, demonstration, etc.
